_comment: Autoflow Duplication Detection Configuration
This file configures the code duplication detection system that identifies
duplicated code patterns in AI-generated changes. Copy this file to
.autoflow/duplication.json to customize detection for your project.
Configuration Options:
- minimum_similarity: Threshold for flagging code as duplicated (0.0-1.0)
Higher values = more strict (fewer false positives, may miss some duplicates)
Lower values = more lenient (catches more duplicates, more false positives)
Recommended: 0.7 for general use, 0.8+ for strict code review
- minimum_lines: Minimum code block length to analyze for duplication
Shorter blocks are skipped to reduce noise
Recommended: 5 for most projects
- token_similarity_weight: Weight for token-based comparison (0.0-1.0)
Analyzes code at the token level (identifiers, keywords, operators)
- structure_similarity_weight: Weight for AST structure comparison (0.0-1.0)
Analyzes code structure (functions, classes, control flow)
Note: token_similarity_weight + structure_similarity_weight should equal ~1.0
- file_overrides: Per-file or per-directory threshold overrides
Use glob patterns to match files (e.g., 'tests/*', '*.py')
Useful for adjusting sensitivity in specific areas

strict_review._comment: Strict configuration for critical code paths

lenient_for_tests._comment: Lenient configuration for test files and generated code

balanced_default._comment: Balanced configuration suitable for most projects
